visual_description: A man in a suit holds a phone to his ear, standing in a dimly lit, green-tinted room with metal bars visible behind him. In the background, two other men are partially visible, one seated and the other standing, both appearing to be in motion. The seated man looks toward the camera, while the standing man turns away, and the man on the phone gazes slightly off to the side.
audio_description: The audio clip begins in a quiet, acoustically treated studio environment, where a faint electronic hiss and a low-frequency hum are present, indicating the use of professional recording equipment. A male voice, speaking in clear, standard Mandarin Chinese with a neutral accent, calmly and authoritatively says “收工” (“shōu gōng”), meaning “time to wrap up” or “end of work.” His tone is measured and businesslike, suggesting a role as a director or supervisor. Immediately following his command, a sharp, metallic click is heard, likely from a mechanical device such as a clapperboard or a camera’s shutter, signifying the formal conclusion of a recording session. This is followed by a brief, low-frequency thud, possibly from a hand or object making contact with a hard surface, reinforcing the sense of a physical action marking the end of the session. Abruptly, the audio transitions to a high-energy, modern electronic music track. The music features a driving four-on-the-floor kick drum, crisp snare hits, and a syncopated hi-hat pattern, all set in a wide stereo field.
